title: Article 131f.  Noncompliance with procedural rules
doc_html:
§231f. Article 131f. Noncompliance with procedural rules

            Any person subject to this Code who:

            (1) is responsible for unnecessary delay in the disposition of any case of a person accused of an offense under this Code; or

            (2) knowingly and intentionally fails to enforce or comply with any provision of this Code regulating the proceedings before, during, or after trial of an accused; shall be punished as a court-martial may direct.

            Acts 1974, No. 621, §1; Acts 2022, No. 672, §1; Redesignated from R.S. 29:198.
doc_text:
§231f.  Article 131f.  Noncompliance with procedural rules
Any person subject to this Code who:
(1)  is responsible for unnecessary delay in the disposition of any case of a person
accused of an offense under this Code; or
(2)  knowingly and intentionally fails to enforce or comply with any provision of this
Code regulating the proceedings before, during, or after trial of an accused; shall be punished
as a court-martial may direct.
Acts 1974, No. 621, §1; Acts 2022, No. 672, §1; Redesignated from R.S. 29:198.
